title: Getting started with regulog
text: regulog provides tamper-evident, hash-chained audit logging R applications. vignette covers core API. Shiny integration, see vignette("shiny-integration").

title: Getting started with regulog
text: Every audit log starts regulog_init(). must supply application name user identity. Optionally provide path persist entries disk newline-delimited JSON file (.rlog). Without path, log -memory — useful development testing, appropriate production regulated deployments.
code: log <- regulog_init(   app     = "clinical-review-tool",   version = "1.2.0",   user    = "jsmith"   # path  = "logs/audit.rlog"  # omit for in-memory only )  log #> <regulog> #>   App:     clinical-review-tool v1.2.0 #>   User:    jsmith #>   Entries: 0 #>   Path:    (in-memory only)

title: Getting started with regulog
text: Use log_action() discrete user actions: approvals, rejections, exports, sign-offs. reason mandatory default. empty blank reason raises error immediately — every entry log must document made.
code: log_action(log,   action = "approved",   object = "dataset_v3.csv",   reason = "QC review complete — no anomalies found" ) #> regulog: logged action 'approved' on 'dataset_v3.csv'  log_action(log,   action = "exported",   object = "dataset_v3.csv",   reason = "Sent to statistical analysis team per SAP section 4.1" ) #> regulog: logged action 'exported' on 'dataset_v3.csv' # This will error: log_action(log, action = "approved", object = "file.csv", reason = "") #> Error in `.assert_reason()`: #> ! A `reason` is required for every logged action or change. #>   Every entry in the audit trail must document why it was made. #>   Provide a justification, e.g.: reason = "Validation metrics passed threshold".

title: Getting started with regulog
text: Use log_change() field value modified. prior new value captured, complete history , became, .
code: log_change(log,   object = "patient_10472",   field  = "dob",   before = "1985-03-01",   after  = "1985-03-11",   reason = "Transcription error — corrected per source document CRF page 14" ) #> regulog: logged change to patient_10472$dob

title: Getting started with regulog
text: verify_log() recomputes every entry hash checks chain links unbroken. Run time, schedule, part handoff. also works directly .rlog file path — R object needed:
code: verify_log(log) #> regulog: Log intact: 3 entries, chain unbroken verify_log("logs/audit.rlog")

title: Getting started with regulog
code: # Simulate tampering — modifying an entry field after the fact log$entries[[1L]]$action <- "ALTERED" verify_log(log) #> Warning: regulog: Log FAILED verification — 1 error(s). First broken entry: #1 #> Entry #1: entry_hash mismatch — content may have been modified

title: Getting started with regulog
text: export_audit_trail() serialises log CSV JSON. Use signed = TRUE regulatory submissions — runs verify_log() stamps chain_intact verified_at every row.
code: # Reset to a clean log for the export example log2 <- regulog_init(app = "clinical-review-tool", version = "1.2.0",                       user = "jsmith") log_action(log2, action = "approved", object = "report_final.pdf",            reason = "All review comments resolved") #> regulog: logged action 'approved' on 'report_final.pdf' log_change(log2, object = "patient_99", field = "visit_date",            before = "2026-04-01", after = "2026-04-11",            reason = "Date transposition error per visit log") #> regulog: logged change to patient_99$visit_date  df <- export_audit_trail(log2, format = "csv", signed = TRUE) df #>   entry_id                   timestamp                  app app_version   user #> 1        1 2026-06-19T16:26:59.329385Z clinical-review-tool       1.2.0 jsmith #> 2        2 2026-06-19T16:26:59.330382Z clinical-review-tool       1.2.0 jsmith #>     type   action           object      field     before      after #> 1 ACTION approved report_final.pdf       <NA>       <NA>       <NA> #> 2 CHANGE     <NA>       patient_99 visit_date 2026-04-01 2026-04-11 #>                                   reason #> 1           All review comments resolved #> 2 Date transposition error per visit log #>                                                         entry_hash #> 1 ed4ecb133dcc64ae1b0e0f232a09927c1e938fb3847a5fedca096964a1c37643 #> 2 598f877417cf893a3f538ecb9f9d1be8ba9dc1f7a8bd6209d7b02ed1a85873ae #>                                                          prev_hash chain_intact #> 1 b3a98cb103b41843401d6e3e32da8ebb09327ac0859217f7b3621bdbf114eb95         TRUE #> 2 ed4ecb133dcc64ae1b0e0f232a09927c1e938fb3847a5fedca096964a1c37643         TRUE #>                   verified_at #> 1 2026-06-19T16:26:59.331566Z #> 2 2026-06-19T16:26:59.331566Z

title: Getting started with regulog
code: export_audit_trail(log2,   format = "csv",   from   = "2026-01-01",   signed = TRUE,   path   = "audit_export.csv" )  # JSON format — useful for archival and system integration export_audit_trail(log2,   format = "json",   signed = TRUE,   path   = "audit_export.json" )

title: Getting started with regulog
text: Entries written newline-delimited JSON (one JSON object per line). format deliberately human-readable — regulator can inspect audit log text editor:
code: {"entry_id":1,"timestamp":"2026-06-18T14:32:01.123456Z","app":"clinical-review-tool","app_version":"1.2.0","user":"jsmith","type":"ACTION","action":"approved","object":"dataset_v3.csv","reason":"QC review complete","prev_hash":"e3b0c4...","entry_hash":"a87ff6..."}

title: Getting started with regulog
text: regulog ships IQ/OQ/PQ qualification scripts inst/validation/. Run sequence qualify package use validated computerised system: Requirements Traceability Matrix (RTM) :
code: source(system.file("validation/IQ_regulog.R", package = "regulog")) source(system.file("validation/OQ_regulog.R", package = "regulog")) source(system.file("validation/PQ_regulog.R", package = "regulog")) rtm_path <- system.file("validation/RTM_regulog.csv", package = "regulog") read.csv(rtm_path)

title: Shiny integration
text: regulog integrates Shiny via regulog_shiny_init(), : Resolves authenticated user session$user (set Shiny Server Pro Posit Connect) — self-reported name Automatically logs session_start session_end events Returns standard regulog object use usual log_action() / log_change() API

title: Shiny integration
code: library(shiny) library(regulog)  server <- function(input, output, session) {    log <- regulog_shiny_init(     session = session,     app     = "clinical-review-tool",     version = "1.2.0",     path    = "logs/audit.rlog"   # shared log file; consider per-session paths   )    observeEvent(input$approve, {     log_action(log,       action = "approved",       object = input$selected_dataset,       reason = input$approval_reason     )   })    observeEvent(input$reject, {     log_action(log,       action = "rejected",       object = input$selected_dataset,       reason = input$rejection_reason     )   }) }  shinyApp(ui = fluidPage(/* ... */), server = server)

title: Shiny integration
text: session$user authenticated identity provided : Shiny Server Pro — OS PAM-authenticated user Posit Connect — Connect account username development (plain shiny::runApp()), session$user typically NULL. regulog_shiny_init() warns falls back Sys.info()[["user"]]. deploy regulated context without authentication configured.
code: Warning: regulog_shiny_init(): session$user is NULL or empty.   Falling back to system user 'jsmith'.   In production, ensure Shiny Server Pro or Posit Connect authentication is configured.

what: Shared log file (simpler, suitable for low-volume apps)
title: Shiny integration
text: sessions append .rlog file. NDJSON append-format makes safe concurrent writes file systems, high-concurrency apps consider per-session files.
code: log <- regulog_shiny_init(   session = session,   app     = "my-app",   version = "1.0.0",   path    = "/opt/logs/my_app_audit.rlog"  # all sessions write to one file )

what: Per-session log files (recommended for multi-user production)
title: Shiny integration
text: session gets file, eliminating concurrency concern.
code: log <- regulog_shiny_init(   session = session,   app     = "my-app",   version = "1.0.0",   path    = sprintf("/opt/logs/my_app_%s_%s.rlog",                     format(Sys.time(), "%Y%m%d_%H%M%S"),                     session$token) )

title: Shiny integration
text: regulog_shiny_init() automatically adds two entries: bracket user-driven entries, giving auditors complete view session.
code: {"entry_id":1, ..., "type":"ACTION", "action":"session_start", "object":"<token>", "reason":"Shiny session opened", ...} {"entry_id":N, ..., "type":"ACTION", "action":"session_end",   "object":"<token>", "reason":"Shiny session closed", ...}

title: Shiny integration
text: apps many loggable inputs, regulog_observer() reduces boilerplate: object reason accept either fixed string reactive().
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {    log <- regulog_shiny_init(session = session, app = "my-app", version = "1.0")    # Instead of writing observeEvent + log_action manually for each input:   regulog_observer(log, session,     eventExpr = input$approve,     action    = "approved",     object    = reactive(input$dataset_name),     reason    = reactive(input$justification)   )    regulog_observer(log, session,     eventExpr = input$lock_db,     action    = "database_locked",     object    = reactive(input$study_id),     reason    = reactive(input$lock_reason)   ) }

title: Shiny integration
text: user edits value UI, capture :
code: # Store the pre-edit value reactively original_dob <- reactiveVal(NULL)  observeEvent(input$patient_selected, {   # Fetch current value from database   original_dob(get_patient_dob(input$patient_id)) })  observeEvent(input$save_edit, {   log_change(log,     object = paste0("patient_", input$patient_id),     field  = "dob",     before = original_dob(),     after  = input$new_dob,     reason = input$edit_reason   )   # Then write to database... })

title: Shiny integration
text: Add download handler allow authorised users export audit trail:
code: output$download_audit <- downloadHandler(   filename = function() {     sprintf("audit_%s_%s.csv", app_name, format(Sys.time(), "%Y%m%d"))   },   content = function(file) {     export_audit_trail(log,       format = "csv",       signed = TRUE,       path   = file     )   } )

title: Shiny integration
code: observeEvent(input$verify_log, {   result <- verify_log(log, verbose = FALSE)   if (result$intact) {     showNotification("Audit log intact.", type = "message")   } else {     showNotification(       paste("Log integrity FAILED. First broken entry:", result$first_broken),       type = "error"     )   } })

code: @Manual{,   title = {regulog: Tamper-Evident Audit Logging for R},   author = {Ndoh Penn},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://repro-stats.github.io/regulog/}, }

title: Tamper-Evident Audit Logging for R
text: Tamper-Evident Audit Logging R regulog provides tamper-evident, hash-chained audit logging R applications. Every log entry cryptographically linked predecessor, making insertions, deletions, modifications detectable. Logs written plain newline-delimited JSON — readable text editor, special tooling required. Use anywhere need reliable record , , .

title: Tamper-Evident Audit Logging for R
code: # Development version remotes::install_github("repro-stats/regulog")

title: Tamper-Evident Audit Logging for R
text: entry stores SHA-256 hash previous entry: Altering field — including timestamp reason — breaks hash chain. verify_log() detects across entries O(n). Entries written flat, one JSON object per line:
code: [GENESIS: h₀] ← [entry 1, h₁ = SHA256(fields|h₀)] ← [entry 2, h₂ = SHA256(fields|h₁)] ← … {   "entry_id":    1,   "timestamp":   "2026-06-19T09:00:21.398739Z",   "app":         "my-app",   "app_version": "1.0.0",   "user":        "jsmith",   "type":        "ACTION",   "action":      "approved",   "object":      "model_v3",   "reason":      "Validation metrics passed agreed threshold",   "prev_hash":   "e3b0c44298fc1c149afb...",   "entry_hash":  "a87ff679a2f3e71d9181..." }

title: Tamper-Evident Audit Logging for R
code: library(regulog)  log <- regulog_init(   app     = "my-app",   version = "1.0.0",   user    = "jsmith",   path    = "logs/audit.rlog"   # omit for in-memory only )

title: Tamper-Evident Audit Logging for R
code: log_action(log,   action = "approved",   object = "model_v3",   reason = "Validation metrics passed agreed threshold" )

title: Tamper-Evident Audit Logging for R
text: reason mandatory default. Every entry must document made.
code: log_change(log,   object = "experiment_7",   field  = "learning_rate",   before = "0.01",   after  = "0.001",   reason = "Loss diverging at 0.01 — reduced per tuning protocol" )

title: Tamper-Evident Audit Logging for R
code: verify_log(log) #> regulog: Log intact: 2 entries, chain unbroken  # Verify directly from the log file — no R object required verify_log("logs/audit.rlog")

title: Tamper-Evident Audit Logging for R
code: # Export to CSV export_audit_trail(log, format = "csv", path = "audit.csv")  # Signed export: verifies the chain and stamps the result on every row export_audit_trail(log,   format = "csv",   signed = TRUE,   path   = "audit_signed.csv" )

title: Tamper-Evident Audit Logging for R
text: regulog integrates directly Shiny. regulog_shiny_init() resolves authenticated user session$user automatically records session start end events.
code: library(shiny) library(regulog)  server <- function(input, output, session) {    log <- regulog_shiny_init(     session = session,     app     = "my-app",     version = "1.0.0",     path    = "logs/audit.rlog"   )    observeEvent(input$approve, {     log_action(log,       action = "approved",       object = input$item_id,       reason = input$reason     )   }) }

title: Tamper-Evident Audit Logging for R
text: regulog includes built-support validated computerised systems operating 21 CFR Part 11 EU Annex 11. Installation, Operational, Performance Qualification (IQ/OQ/PQ) scripts Requirements Traceability Matrix included can run formally qualify regulog use regulated environment:
code: source(system.file("validation/IQ_regulog.R", package = "regulog")) source(system.file("validation/OQ_regulog.R", package = "regulog")) source(system.file("validation/PQ_regulog.R", package = "regulog"))  # Requirements Traceability Matrix read.csv(system.file("validation/RTM_regulog.csv", package = "regulog"))

title: Tamper-Evident Audit Logging for R
text: silent failures — every log_* call succeeds errors explicitly Reason mandatory — default; enforced R level, convention Human-readable — flat NDJSON; inspectable text editor Minimal dependencies — digest jsonlite Append-— log files never overwritten, extended Verifiable offline — verify_log() works file path R object needed

title: Export the audit trail — export_audit_trail
text: Serialises log entries CSV JSON, optional date filtering. Use signed = TRUE run chain verification stamp integrity result export — useful handoffs, audits, archival.

title: Export the audit trail — export_audit_trail
code: export_audit_trail(   log,   format = c("csv", "json"),   from = NULL,   to = NULL,   path = NULL,   signed = FALSE,   include_genesis = FALSE )

title: Export the audit trail — export_audit_trail
text: log regulog object path .rlog file. format Character. "csv" "json". Character NULL. Include entries date (ISO-8601, e.g. "2026-01-01"). Character NULL. Include entries date. path Character NULL. Output file path. NULL, returns data without writing disk. signed Logical. TRUE, verify chain include chain_intact verified_at fields export. include_genesis Logical. Include genesis record. Default FALSE.

title: Export the audit trail — export_audit_trail
text: data frame (CSV) list (JSON), invisibly.

title: Export the audit trail — export_audit_trail
code: log <- regulog_init(app = "my-app", user = "jsmith") log_action(log, action = "approved", object = "model_v3",            reason = "Metrics passed threshold") #> regulog: logged action 'approved' on 'model_v3' df <- export_audit_trail(log, format = "csv")  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ export_audit_trail(log,   format = "csv",   from   = "2026-01-01",   signed = TRUE,   path   = "audit_export.csv" ) } # }

what: Log a discrete action in the audit trail — log_action
title: Log a discrete action in the audit trail — log_action
text: Records user action (approval, rejection, sign-, deployment, export, etc.) tamper-evident, hash-chained entry audit log.

title: Log a discrete action in the audit trail — log_action
code: log_action(log, action, object, reason, user = log$user)

title: Log a discrete action in the audit trail — log_action
text: log regulog object regulog_init(). action Character. happened (e.g. "approved", "deployed", "rejected", "exported"). object Character. happened (filename, model ID, record ID, pipeline step, etc.). reason Character. Mandatory. happened. default. user Character. Override session user entry. Defaults user set regulog_init().

title: Log a discrete action in the audit trail — log_action
text: regulog object, invisibly (pipe-friendly).

title: Log a discrete action in the audit trail — log_action
code: log <- regulog_init(app = "my-app", user = "jsmith") log_action(log,   action = "approved",   object = "model_v3",   reason = "Validation metrics passed agreed threshold" ) #> regulog: logged action 'approved' on 'model_v3'

what: Log a before/after field change in the audit trail — log_change
title: Log a before/after field change in the audit trail — log_change
text: Records data modification prior new values. Use whenever specific field record changed need full history , became, .

title: Log a before/after field change in the audit trail — log_change
code: log_change(log, object, field, before, after, reason, user = log$user)

title: Log a before/after field change in the audit trail — log_change
text: log regulog object regulog_init(). object Character. record modified (e.g. "user_42", "config.yaml", "experiment_7"). field Character. field changed (e.g. "status", "threshold"). value change (coerced character). value change (coerced character). reason Character. Mandatory. change made. default. user Character. Override session user. Defaults session user.

title: Log a before/after field change in the audit trail — log_change
text: regulog object, invisibly.

title: Log a before/after field change in the audit trail — log_change
code: log <- regulog_init(app = "my-app", user = "jsmith") log_change(log,   object = "experiment_7",   field  = "learning_rate",   before = "0.01",   after  = "0.001",   reason = "Loss diverging at 0.01 — reduced per tuning protocol" ) #> regulog: logged change to experiment_7$learning_rate

what: Initialise a regulog audit log session — regulog_init
title: Initialise a regulog audit log session — regulog_init
text: Creates new audit log session object. Subsequent calls log_action() log_change() append hash-chained entries. path supplied, entries written newline-delimited JSON file (.rlog).

title: Initialise a regulog audit log session — regulog_init
code: regulog_init(   app,   version = "unknown",   user = Sys.info()[["user"]],   path = NULL,   hash_algo = "sha256" )

title: Initialise a regulog audit log session — regulog_init
text: app Character. Application system name (e.g. "data-pipeline", "review-tool", "ml-trainer"). version Character. Application version string. user Character. Identity acting user. Defaults Sys.info()[["user"]]. Shiny, pass session$user. path Character NULL. Path persistent storage. NULL, log -memory (suitable development / testing). hash_algo Character. Algorithm passed digest::digest(). Defaults "sha256". change log file use.

title: Initialise a regulog audit log session — regulog_init
text: S3 object class "regulog" (environment).

title: Initialise a regulog audit log session — regulog_init
text: Every entry written disk JSON object single line:   flat structure intentional: log inspectable text editor, without specialist software.
code: {   "entry_id":    1,   "timestamp":   "2026-06-18T14:32:01.123456Z",   "app":         "my-app",   "app_version": "1.0.0",   "user":        "jsmith",   "action":      "approved",   "object":      "model_v3",   "reason":      "Validation metrics passed threshold",   "prev_hash":   "e3b0c44298fc1c149afb...",   "entry_hash":  "a87ff679a2f3e71d9181..." }

title: Initialise a regulog audit log session — regulog_init
text: entry_hash SHA-256 canonical string encoding fields plus prev_hash. Altering field — including timestamp reason — invalidates hash subsequent chain links, detectable verify_log().

title: Initialise a regulog audit log session — regulog_init
code: log <- regulog_init(   app     = "my-app",   version = "1.0.0",   user    = "jsmith" ) log #> <regulog> #>   App:     my-app v1.0.0 #>   User:    jsmith #>   Entries: 0 #>   Path:    (in-memory only)

what: Create a logging observer for a reactive Shiny input — regulog_observer
title: Create a logging observer for a reactive Shiny input — regulog_observer
text: Wraps shiny::observeEvent() log action whenever eventExpr fires. Reduces boilerplate many UI events need audited.

title: Create a logging observer for a reactive Shiny input — regulog_observer
code: regulog_observer(log, session, eventExpr, action, object, reason, ...)

title: Create a logging observer for a reactive Shiny input — regulog_observer
text: log regulog object. session Shiny session object. eventExpr Reactive expression observe. action Character. Action label. object Character reactive. object acted upon. reason Character reactive. Business justification. ... Additional arguments passed log_action().

title: Create a logging observer for a reactive Shiny input — regulog_observer
text: Shiny observer (invisibly).

title: Create a logging observer for a reactive Shiny input — regulog_observer
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ regulog_observer(log, session,   eventExpr = input$approve,   action    = "approved",   object    = reactive(input$selected_dataset),   reason    = reactive(input$reason_text) ) } # }

what: Initialise a regulog session inside a Shiny server — regulog_shiny_init
title: Initialise a regulog session inside a Shiny server — regulog_shiny_init
text: thin wrapper around regulog_init() resolves authenticated user session$user (set Shiny Server Pro / Posit Connect) automatically logs session_start session_end events.

title: Initialise a regulog session inside a Shiny server — regulog_shiny_init
code: regulog_shiny_init(   session,   app,   version = "unknown",   path = NULL,   hash_algo = "sha256" )

title: Initialise a regulog session inside a Shiny server — regulog_shiny_init
text: session Shiny session object. app Character. Application name. version Character. Application version. path Character NULL. Persistent log file path. NULL, per-session temp file created (suitable development ; logs lost session ends). hash_algo Character. Hashing algorithm. Defaults "sha256".

title: Initialise a regulog session inside a Shiny server — regulog_shiny_init
text: regulog object log tied authenticated session user.

title: Initialise a regulog session inside a Shiny server — regulog_shiny_init
text: session$user authenticated identity set Shiny Server Pro Posit Connect. open deployments authentication configured, NULL "". regulog_shiny_init() falls back Sys.info()[["user"]] case, warning.

title: Initialise a regulog session inside a Shiny server — regulog_shiny_init
text: Two entries added automatically: session_start regulog_shiny_init() called session_end via shiny::onSessionEnded() bracket user-driven entries, giving regulators complete picture session lifecycle.

title: Initialise a regulog session inside a Shiny server — regulog_shiny_init
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {    log <- regulog_shiny_init(     session = session,     app     = "my-app",     version = "1.2.0",     path    = "/logs/audit.rlog"   )    observeEvent(input$approve, {     log_action(log,       action = "approved",       object = input$dataset,       reason = input$reason     )   }) }

title: Initialise a regulog session inside a Shiny server — regulog_shiny_init
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(shiny) library(regulog)  server <- function(input, output, session) {   log <- regulog_shiny_init(     session = session,     app     = "my-app",     version = "1.0.0",     path    = "logs/audit.rlog"   )   observeEvent(input$submit, {     log_action(log,       action = "submitted",       object = input$form_id,       reason = input$justification     )   }) }  shinyApp(ui = fluidPage(), server = server) } # }

what: Verify the integrity of an audit log chain — verify_log
title: Verify the integrity of an audit log chain — verify_log
text: Recomputes every entry hash confirms matches stored value, prev_hash matches predecessor's entry_hash. discrepancy indicates tampering corruption.

title: Verify the integrity of an audit log chain — verify_log
code: verify_log(log, verbose = TRUE)

title: Verify the integrity of an audit log chain — verify_log
text: log regulog object character path .rlog file. verbose Logical. Print summary. Defaults TRUE.

title: Verify the integrity of an audit log chain — verify_log
text: list (invisibly) components: intact Logical. TRUE chain unbroken. n_entries Integer. Number data entries verified (genesis excluded). first_broken Integer NA. entry_id first invalid entry. errors Character vector error descriptions.

title: Verify the integrity of an audit log chain — verify_log
text: entry (excluding genesis record): Reconstruct hash_input stored fields canonical order. Recompute digest(hash_input, algo = hash_algo). Assert computed == entry$entry_hash (content integrity). Assert entry$prev_hash == entry[-1]$entry_hash (chain continuity). Step 3 failure: entry's content modified writing. Step 4 failure: entries inserted, deleted, reordered.

title: Verify the integrity of an audit log chain — verify_log
code: log <- regulog_init(app = "my-app", user = "jsmith") log_action(log, action = "approved", object = "file.csv",            reason = "Review complete") #> regulog: logged action 'approved' on 'file.csv' verify_log(log) #> regulog: Log intact: 1 entry, chain unbroken #> v Log intact: 1 entry, chain unbroken

title: regulog (development version)
text: regulog_init() — initialise hash-chained audit log session. Writes genesis record disk path supplied. log_action() — log discrete user action. reason mandatory default — every entry must document made. log_change() — log /field change mandatory reason. Captures changed, changed , , . verify_log() — recompute every entry hash confirm chain links. Accepts live regulog object .rlog file path. export_audit_trail() — export entries CSV JSON, optional date filtering. signed = TRUE runs verification stamps chain_intact verified_at export. regulog_shiny_init() — Shiny integration. Resolves session$user authenticated identity instruments session_start / session_end events automatically. regulog_observer() — convenience wrapper around shiny::observeEvent() logs action every trigger. inst/validation/ — IQ/OQ/PQ qualification scripts Requirements Traceability Matrix (RTM) 21 CFR Part 11 EU Annex 11.
